briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of Synapse, highlighting critical risks in the fintech banking-as-a-service model.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a sovereign payment network, signalling a major shift away from US-dominated card infrastructure.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competitive dynamics in the payments industry.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of open banking firm Credit Kudos signals the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial data and credit services.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Wealthsimple unveils new family and business products as it broadens banking push](https://betakit.com/wealthsimple-unveils-new-family-and-business-products-as-it-broadens-banking-push/)** — *BetaKit* — Wealthsimple is expanding beyond individual investing by launching banking products tailored to families and businesses, deepening its push into everyday financial services.

- **[Wealthsimple wants to expand its banking to kids and businesses](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/wealthsimple-launching-kids-businesses-9.7207296?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's leading fintech platform is broadening its reach by introducing banking features for children and business customers.

- **[What tech CEOs want from the new federal AI strategy](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bakx-ai-strategy-federal-tech-data-9.7206174?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canadian tech executives are pressing the federal government on data policy and investment priorities as Ottawa shapes its national AI strategy.

- **[Canada injects millions into AI research program amid "global war" for talent](https://betakit.com/canada-injects-millions-into-ai-research-program-amid-global-war-for-talent/)** — *BetaKit* — The federal government is committing new funding to AI research as Canada competes internationally to attract and retain top technology talent.

- **[Intuit lays off 17 percent of its global workforce](https://betakit.com/intuit-lays-off-17-percent-of-its-global-workforce/)** — *BetaKit* — Financial software giant Intuit is cutting roughly 17% of its global staff, a move with potential ripple effects for Canadian small business and fintech tooling markets.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[From the ashes of Ubisoft Halifax, an indie studio rises to revive Atlantic Canada's gaming industry](https://betakit.com/from-the-ashes-of-ubisoft-halifax-an-indie-studio-rises-to-revive-atlantic-canadas-gaming-industry/)** — *BetaKit* — A new indie studio emerging from the closure of Ubisoft Halifax reflects the broader resilience and entrepreneurial energy in Atlantic Canada's tech ecosystem — a dynamic relevant to fintech founders building in the region.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's foundational push toward open banking and away from screen scraping remains essential context as Atlantic fintechs prepare for the national Consumer-Driven Banking framework now taking shape.
